title: viguiera benthamii
intro: Viguiera benthamii, also known as the Bentham's tree-mallow or tree-mallow, is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues such as diarrhea, constipation, and dysentery, due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The health benefits of Viguiera benthamii include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to make ointments and salves for topical application.
